file_insights.changes: Replaced generic error context with domain-specific helper function call
file_insights.impact: Call site now uses require_card() instead of find_card() with manual context, improving error handling consistency

file_insights.changes: Added new public function require_card() that wraps find_card() with helpful error messaging
file_insights.impact: Provides user-friendly error messages that guide users to next steps (bop list command)

patterns_discovered.description: Creating domain-specific Result-returning wrapper functions that enhance generic Option-returning lookups with contextual error messages
patterns_discovered.example: require_card() wraps find_card() and adds helpful hint about 'bop list'

patterns_discovered.description: Using .with_context(|| format!(...)) to include runtime values in error messages without String allocation when no error occurs
patterns_discovered.example: format!("card not found: {}\nTry: bop list", id)

patterns_discovered.description: Rust convention of providing both Option-returning (find_*) and Result-returning (require_*) variants for flexibility
patterns_discovered.example: find_card returns Option, require_card returns Result with enhanced context

gotchas_discovered.context: Error messages with newlines embedded in format strings ensure suggestions are visible in terminal output
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Used explicit \n in format! macro to ensure line break appears correctly in error display

gotchas_discovered.context: Function naming (require_* vs find_*) indicates different return types (Result vs Option) and error handling semantics
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Naming is consistent with Rust conventions - requires developers to use correct variant for their use case

discoveries.approach_details: Implemented a targeted improvement to user-facing error messages by creating a wrapper function that provides actionable guidance. The change maintains backward compatibility (find_card still exists) while providing a better error experience through require_card().

recommendations.recommendation: Audit other 'not found' error paths in the CLI and apply the require_* pattern consistently for uniform UX
Consider documenting the require_* vs find_* pattern convention in CONTRIBUTING guide for consistency across codebase
If error telemetry exists, monitor which hints are most commonly encountered to validate/improve suggested commands
